observations.recommendation:
Management ensures that:

The Inventory Committee ensure the conduct of the required annual physical inventory of PPEs; submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team, based on the results of physical inventory of PPEs; expedite and complete the process of consolidation of all the necessary reports relative to the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances; submit the pertinent reports and documents relative to the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE account to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation; and the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustment in the PPE accounts and fairly present the balances in the LGU books, based on the reports and documents prepared by the Inventory Committee.

Management ensures that:

The Municipal Treasurer, being the head of the Inventory Committee, properly plans and ensures the conduct of periodic physical count of inventories, maintain stock cards and prepare RIS and SSMI, pursuant to Sections 114, 121 and 124 of the MNGAS.

The Municipal Accountant assigns specific accounting personnel who shall be in-charge in maintaining the stock cards for inventories in order to comply with Section 114 of the MNGAS.

The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year after due consideration of payments for delinquent taxes, at the beginning of the year, in accordance with Section 20 of the Manual on NGAS, Volume 1.

The Municipal Accountant immediately record the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the certified list furnished by the Municipal Treasurer to timely recognize the tax receivables.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers on the immediate submission of liquidation reports on the financial assistance given by the municipal government.

Management fully utilizes the 20 per cent Development Fund pursuant to the intents of DBM-DILG-DOF JMC No.1 by ensuring that only PPAs which are ready for implementation are included in the plan and that procurement timelines are followed so as to avoid delays in project implementation.

MDRRMO include only those projects and activities in the LDRRMP which are deemed to be of high priority and likelihood of implementation, in order to ensure that management meet the objectives of R.A. 10121 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-01.
Management to implement the programs/projects/ activities funded under the continuing appropriations consistent with Sections 305(l) 306(e) of R.A.7160.

To submit a written explanation pertaining to the non-utilization of the unexpended amount of funds received from LBP and DBP.

That the MSWDO and MDDRMO immediately identify the affected families of Typhoon Odette who were not yet given any financial assistance by the LGU or are in most need of any financial assistance and immediately facilitate the distribution of financial assistance to these families.

Management facilitates the implementation of the projects that were not yet implemented. For those partially implemented PPAs, we recommend that management monitor its implementation to be able to complete the projects according to the specified contract duration, pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-1.

Management submit explanation on the afore-cited observation. We also recommended that management, henceforth, closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned programs/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre-disaster preparedness so that intended purpose is attained/achieved as required in Section 21 of RA 10121 and Section 4 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 of NDRRMC and DBM.

Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the processing of land titling. It is also recommended that the management provide funds therefor in order to expedite the processing of titles and inform and furnish the Audit Team, any data and documents as to the status of land titling. Moreover, it is recommended that management ensure that the purpose for every land acquisition has been met for the benefit of the general public.

Issuance of confirmation letters and if duly confirmed send collection letters to agencies/persons concerned as proofs that the municipality has exerted efforts/exhausted all means to collect these receivables that have become dormant.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee ensure the conduct of the required annual physical inventory of PPEs, submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team, based on the results of physical inventory of PPEs, expedite and complete the process of consolidation of all the necessary reports relative to the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, submit the pertinent reports and documents relative to the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE account to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the necessary adjustment in the PPE accounts and fairly present the balances in the LGU books, based on the reports and documents prepared by the Inventory Committee and ensure that the total balance of PPELCs tally with the controlling PPE accounts in the General Ledger, to establish proper reconciliation of records.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, being the head of the Inventory Committee,  properly plan and ensure the conduct of periodic physical count of inventories, maintain stock cards and prepare RIS and SSMI, pursuant to Sections 114, 121 and 124 of the Manual on the NGAS. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant assign specific accounting personnel who shall be in-charge in maintaining the stock cards for inventories in order to comply with Section 114 of the Manual on the NGAS.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare a Report of Physical Count of Local Road Network (RPCLRN) using the prescribed format per COA Circular No. 2015-008.
We also recommended that the GSO maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all the roads and their components.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year after due consideration of payments for delinquent taxes, at the beginning of the year, in accordance with Section 20 of the MNGAS, Volume 1.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately record the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the certified list furnished by the Municipal Treasurer to timely recognize the tax receivables.

Submit an explanation on the: (a) delayed and incomplete submission of documents required for the release of the last tranche of funds for the Dagohoy Resettlement Project - Phase 1 which resulted in the non-payment of the contractor in the amount of ₱995,472.83; (b) delayed approval of the Code of Policies on the Beneficiary Selection for the Dagohoy Resettlement Project - Phase 1; and (c) insufficient action on the part of the management which resulted in the lapse of NHA fund allocation amounting to ₱10 million.

Expedite the processing of required documents and reports and intensify the coordination efforts with the NHA in order to fully implement the Dagohoy Resettlement Project – Phase 1 and 2 and immediately deliver services and benefits to the intended beneficiaries.

The Municipal Engineer submit the supporting documents for the Request for Time Extension, specifically the tabulation of unworkable days and weather charts for those days, being the recommendatory LGU official for the issuance of Time Extension.

Management requires the contractor to expedite the project completion, considering the issuance of ECC by the DENR.

The Municipal Accountant ascertain any unwarranted delays and compute liquidated damages, whenever applicable, which shall be deducted from payments due to the contractor.

Municipal Treasurer enforce more stringent measures and avail of other remedies in order to collect delinquent taxes pursuant to Sections 256 and 247 of R.A.7160, to facilitate implementation of programs and projects of the LGU which shall be financed out of collections from tax delinquencies.

We recommended that management facilitate the implementation of the projects that were not yet implemented. For those partially implemented PPAs, we recommend that management monitor its implementation to be able to complete the projects according to the specified contract duration, pursuant to DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer enforce more stringent measures and avail of other remedies in order to collect delinquent taxes pursuant to Sections 256 and 247 of R.A.7160, to facilitate implementation of programs and projects of the LGU which shall be financed out of collections from tax delinquencies.

We recommended that management submit explanation on the afore-cited observation. We also recommended that management, henceforth, closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned programs/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre-disaster preparedness so that intended purpose is attained/achieved as required in Section 21 of RA 10121 and Section 4 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 of NDRRMC and DBM.

12. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the processing of land titling. It is also recommended that the management provide funds therefor in order to expedite the processing of titles and inform and furnish the Audit Team, any data and documents as to the status of land titling. Moreover, it is recommended that management ensure that the purpose for every land acquisition has been met for the benefit of the general public.

We recommended issuance of confirmation letters and if duly confirmed send collection letters to agencies/persons concerned as proofs that the municipality has exerted efforts/exhausted all means to collect these receivables that have become dormant.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers on the immediate submission of liquidation reports on the financial assistance given by the municipal government.

6.6 	We recommended that Management fully utilize the 20 per cent Development Fund pursuant to the intents of DBM-DILG-DOF JMC No.1 by ensuring that only PPAs which are ready for implementation are included in the plan and that procurement timelines are followed so as to avoid delays in project implementation.

7.6 	We recommended and Management agreed that the MDRRMO include only those projects and activities in the LDRRMP which are deemed to be of high priority and likelihood of implementation, in order to ensure that management meet the objectives of R.A. 10121 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-01.
8.6	We recommended and Management to implement the programs/projects/ activities funded under the continuing appropriations consistent with Sections 305(l) 306(e) of R.A.7160.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Assessor to exert efforts in order to facilitate the conduct timely general or particular revision of real property assessments as required under Section 219 of R.A. 7160 and Section I, Chapter III of the Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations so as to enable collection of additional real property taxes which could be derived from the revised real property assessments.

We recommended that management formulate innovative schemes and exercise robust measures to achieve target revenues and maximize collection thereof in consonance with Section 305 (g), Chapter I, Section 444 (a) and (3) Chapter III of the Republic Act (R.A.) No.7160 and keep up with the provisions of DOF D.O. No.006-2015 in order to achieve satisfactory performance in revenue generation.

We recommended that management observe Section 4.7 of COA Circular No.2022-004 on the preparation of complete and proper documentation on the issuance/turnover/transfer/return of property items in order to provide adequate controls over the issued semi-expendable items and establish custody and accountability therefor.

We recommended that management comply with the provisions of Items 3.0 and 4.2 of NDDRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No.2014-1 as to the complete creation of the four (4) mandatory positions in the LDRRMO so as to ensure that sufficient number of competent personnel are employed who shall be responsible in areas of: (1) administration and training; (2) research and planning; and (3) operations and warning.

We recommended that management revisit the current regular plantilla items of the LGU and propose/create another plantilla position when necessary to avoid hiring of contract of service and job order personnel to perform the duties and responsibilities of a regular employee, in compliance with Section 2, Rule XI of CSC MC No. 40, series of 1998 and Sections 7.1 and 7.2 of CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017.

We recommended that management closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned programs/ projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness so that intended purpose is attained/achieved as required in– Section 21 of RA 10121 and Section 4 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 of NDRRMC and DBM.

We recommended that the management observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-009 on the receipt, recording, distribution, reporting and direct release of donations in-kind and relief goods to the intended beneficiaries. Likewise, we recommended that the MSWDO and MDRRMO submit to the Audit Team the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for the received donations in-kind and actual list of beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.2020-009. Also, we recommended that management secure documents that would determine the cost of donated items received from the Provincial Social Welfare and Development Office in order to provide basis on the recording of remaining undistributed items, if any.

Forgoing considered, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local GAD Focal Person through the Municipal Planning and Development Office to collect and keep sex-disaggregated data and to perform gender analysis so as to concretely define its gender issues, to accordingly design programs and activities that could most address them and ultimately to ensure that its fund is utilized to the purpose it is intended.
13. We recommended that management facilitate the creation and maintenance of an active official LGU website to comply with Section 1 of E.O.662, in the spirit of transparency of procurement process.

14. We recommended that management prioritize and consider filling–up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents. Further, personnel movement has to be effected in order to acknowledge the exemplary performance of employees by way of promotion to higher rank thereby allowing the entry of new applicants for lower-level positions.

15. We recommended that management provide a venue to discuss with the municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its employees are able to receive a minimum of ₱3,500 take home pay per month in pursuance of Section 52 of the 2015 GAA.  Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO monitor payroll deductions and refuse to sign certifications for application of loans should an employee net pay fall below ₱3,500.00.

We recommended that management submit the aforementioned lacking documents and provide explanation on the discrepancies of submitted documents. Also, instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to furnish the Audit Team with the copies of perfected contracts together with the complete set of the integral parts/supporting documents thereof to enable the conduct of timely review, validation/inspection thereof, pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002.

We recommended that management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to conduct pre-procurement conferences pursuant to the provisions of Section 20 of the R-IRR of RA No. 9184 and ensure submission of its minutes to the Audit Team in order to attain transparency in the procurement process.
We recommended that the BAC strictly observe the mandatory posting of all notices and contracts awarded pursuant to Section 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the R-IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to the concerned Punong Barangays and Barangay Treasurers on the immediate submission of liquidation reports on the financial assistance given by the municipal government. Likewise, it is recommended that management constantly remind the concerned barangay officials of their responsibility to submit liquidation reports as recipient of funds transferred from the municipality, in order to fairly present the account Due from Local Government Units in the financial statements and provide proof that the purpose for the fund transfer has already been attained. Also, refrain from extending grant to recipients with unliquidated financial assistance.

We recommended that management  closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned programs/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness so that  intended purpose  is attained/achieved as required in– Section 21 of RA 10121 and Section 4 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 of NDRRMC and DBM.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person of the Municipality      immediately submit the GAD Accomplishment Report to COA. Further, we recommended that the Chairperson of GAD Focal Point System regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to timely   and properly evaluate those activities yet for implementation before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the municipal employees and general public pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

We recommended that the management observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-009 on the receipt, recording, distribution, reporting and direct release of donations in-kind and relief goods to the intended beneficiaries. Likewise, we recommended that the MSWDO and MDRRMO submit to the Audit Team the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for the received donations in-kind and actual list of beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.2020-009. Also, we recommended that management secure documents that would determine the cost of donated items received from the Provincial Social Welfare and Development Office in order to provide basis on the recording of remaining undistributed items, if any.

We recommended that management submit an explanation on the cause of delay of project completion as well as documents that support the explanation. Furthermore, we recommended that management furnish the Audit Team copies of communication or development, if any, on the implementation of these projects. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide computation for liquidated damages to account for unwarranted delays and withhold the amount from payments due to contractor/supplier. Lastly, we recommended that henceforth, management ensure completion of implemented projects within the time frame set in order to timely deliver the benefits these projects will have provide the public and to avoid accumulation of projects to be implemented and constantly monitored.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | Management submitted explanation for the cause of delay of project implementation and appropriate supporting documents. Also, management ensured that projects were completed as soon as possible.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, use the account Advances to Special Disbursing Officer in recording the grant of cash advances for special purpose/time-bound undertakings so as to properly recognize accounts in
the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | Management effected the suggested journal entries.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
a. Adopt the perpetual inventory method for the Food Supplies, Drugs and Medicine Supplies and Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies by setting up Inventory Accounts.
b. Coordinate directly with the concerned departments for the submission of duly acknowledged Requisition and Issue Slips for the withdrawal/issuance of supplies and the Monthly Report of the Food Supplies, Drugs and Medicines, and Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies issued and its supporting documents, in order to establish inventories on hand at year-end.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | Starting CY 2021, management recorded the receipt of various supplies in their respective inventory accounts, thereby adopting the perpetual inventory method.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management henceforth, ensure that distribution of food packs is properly accounted for and documented. Further, we recommended submission of the distribution list showing the contents of the packs for the beneficiaries. Also, we recommended that management see to it that payments for purchases of relief packs are properly supported with complete documentation to establish the propriety, validity and completeness of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | Management has included additional information such as the exact contents of the goods distributed as well as date of distribution and receipt of goods in the preparation of relief goods distribution list.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the lacking documents and provide explanation on noted deficiencies so as to support the propriety of the constructed infrastructure assets and establish transparency and accountability in the procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | Management has submitted lacking documents and explanation addressing the noted deficiencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management provide proof that the procured Rapid Test Kits were from FDA-accredited manufacturers. Henceforth, management is enjoined to take note on government accreditations especially on health products to ensure the health and safety of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | In the procurement of Rapid Test Kits, management has included in the supporting documents additional documentation certifying that the manufacturers of the said kits were accredited with the FDA.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management provide justification through written
explanation or provide additional documentary requirement on this noted observation. Moreover, we recommended that management adhere to the GPPB Circulars and Advisories for procurement procedures and requirements.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | Management provided the Audit Team with the lacking supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management, henceforth, prepare check payments for goods and services instead of resorting to cash advance or reimbursement basis in keeping with the intents of COA Circular No.97-002 and to ensure withholding of taxes so as help the government in its efficient and effective collection of taxes.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | Management submitted an explanation regarding the special circumstances which led to this transaction being done through reimbursement basis. They assured the Audit Team that they would undertake additional measures in the future in case similar conditions occur again.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the persons who will prepare the purchase requests, request for quotations, purchase orders, and inspection and acceptance report to provide narrative description, size/dimension/volume, model, type, classification and other necessary descriptions of supplies to be procured so as to guarantee delivery of goods which meets the effectiveness or performance requirements of the end-user. Also, we recommended that Management submit a written explanation for the lapse in providing item descriptions on various purchases.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | CY 2020 AAR | Management practiced diligence in the preparation of supporting documents for procurement, thereby providing additional details to items to be procured, such as, but not limited to, narrative description, size, dimension, volume, model, type, classification and other descriptions when deemed necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the excessive grant of Subsistence Allowance to the MSWDO and PHWs at a fixed amount of ₱1,500.00 and instead, abide by the prescribed rates of Magna Carta Benefit for Public Health Workers and Public Social Workers as stipulated in DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2012 and DBM-DSWD Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2018. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant be kept abreast of latest issuances of applicable government agencies to ensure that funds are utilized in accordance with relevant rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | Starting CY 2021, management paid out subsistence allowance to MSWDO and Public Health Workers as prescribed under Section 6.2 of DBM-DSWD Joint Circular (JC) No. 1 series of 2018 and Section 8.3 of DBM-DOH JC No. 1 series of 2012.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the excessive grant of Hazard Pay to the MSWDO at a fixed amount of ₱4,061.40 and instead, follow the computation of Hazard Pay per Section 5.3 of the DBM-DSWD JC No. 1 series of 2018. Likewise, we also recommended that the Municipal Accountant be kept abreast of latest issuances of applicable government agencies to ensure that funds are utilized in accordance with relevant rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | Starting CY 2021, management paid out hazard pay using the computation prescribed under Section 5.3 of the DBM-DSWD JC No. 1 series of 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to properly plan ahead and determine the schedule for physical count per department in order to prioritize and fully realize the conduct of a complete physical count of the supplies inventory and property, plant and equipment of the LGU. Likewise, we recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare the RPCPPE and RPCI and submit copies thereof to COA in order to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. Also, We recommended that henceforth, accounting records be properly and timely reconciled with the results of the physical count in order to effect the necessary adjustments, to fairly present these accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019 AAR | Management was able to conduct full inventory count last September 2021 and submitted updated RPCPPEs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management immediately cause the issuance of corresponding SB resolution for unimplemented PPAs with generic appropriation and hasten project implementation by proceeding with the procurement activities as specified in the Annual Budget. For those partially implemented PPAs, we recommended that Management monitor its implementation to be able to complete the projects according to the specified contract duration, pursuant to Item 5.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No.2017-1.
observations.managementAction: CY 2019 AAR | Management have substantially implemented the specified projects/programs/ activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management impose liquidated damages for late deliveries pursuant to the afore-stated provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9184 to prevent suppliers from neglecting their responsibility to comply with the conditions of the contract and to avoid delay in the implementation of projects.
observations.managementAction: CY 2020 AAR | Management noted the finding and pledged to effect the recommended change on how to calculate the number of days elapsed from receipt of Purchase Order by the supplier.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the lacking documents and provide explanation on noted deficiencies so as to support the propriety of the constructed infrastructure assets and establish transparency and accountability in the procurement activities.

We recommended that management submit an explanation on the cause of delay of project completion as well as documents that support the explanation. Furthermore, we recommended that management furnish the Audit Team copies of communication or development, if any, on the implementation of these projects. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide computation for liquidated damages to account for unwarranted delays and withhold the amount from payments due to contractor/supplier. Lastly, we recommended that henceforth, management ensure completion of implemented projects within the time frame set in order to timely deliver the benefits these projects will have provide the public and to avoid accumulation of projects to be implemented and constantly monitored.

We recommended that management:
a.	Adopt the perpetual inventory method for the Food Supplies, Drugs and Medicine Supplies and Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies by setting up Inventory Accounts.
b.	Coordinate directly with the concerned departments for the submission of duly acknowledged Requisition and Issue Slips for the withdrawal/issuance of supplies and the Monthly Report of the Food Supplies, Drugs and Medicines, and Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies issued and its supporting documents, in order to establish inventories on hand at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, use the account Advances to Special Disbursing Officer in recording the grant of cash advances for special purpose/time-bound undertakings so as to properly recognize accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that management stop the excessive grant of Hazard Pay to the MSWDO at a fixed amount of ₱4,061.40 and instead, follow the computation of Hazard Pay per Section 5.3 of the DBM-DSWD JC No. 1 series of 2018. Likewise, we also recommend that the Municipal Accountant be kept abreast of latest issuances of applicable government agencies to ensure that funds are utilized in accordance with relevant rules and regulations.

We recommended that management stop the excessive grant of Subsistence Allowance to the MSWDO and PHWs at a fixed amount of ₱1,500.00 and instead, abide by the prescribed rates of Magna Carta Benefit for Public Health Workers and Public Social Workers as stipulated in DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2012 and DBM-DSWD Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2018.  Further, we also recommend that the Municipal Accountant be kept abreast of latest issuances of applicable government agencies to ensure that funds are utilized in accordance with relevant rules and regulations.

We recommended that management henceforth, ensure that distribution of food packs is properly accounted for and documented. Further, we recommended submission of the distribution list showing the contents of the packs for the beneficiaries.  Also, we recommended that management see to it that payments for purchases of relief packs are properly supported with complete documentation to establish the propriety, validity and completeness of the transactions, in observance of the pertinent provisions of DBM LBC No. 125.

We recommended that management provide proof that the procured Rapid Test Kits were from FDA-accredited manufacturers so as to ensure proper utilization of BGCM fund.  Henceforth, management is enjoined to take note on government accreditations especially on health products to ensure the health and safety of the constituents.

We recommended that Management impose liquidated damages for late deliveries pursuant to the afore-stated provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9184 to prevent suppliers from neglecting their responsibility to comply with the conditions of the contract and to avoid delay in the implementation of projects and ensure proper utilization and accounting of BGCM fund.

We recommended that management, henceforth, prepare check payments for goods and services payable to the supplier or service provider instead of resorting to cash advance or reimbursement basis in keeping with the intents of COA Circular No.97-002 and to ensure withholding of taxes so as help the government in its efficient and effective collection of taxes.
We recommended that management observe the provisions of                                 GPPB Circular 01-2020 so as to guarantee procurement of goods which is most advantageous to the government.

We recommended that Management direct the persons who will prepare the purchase requests, request for quotations, purchase orders, and inspection and acceptance report to provide narrative description, size/dimension/volume, model, type, classification and other necessary descriptions of supplies to be procured so as to guarantee delivery of goods which meets the effectiveness or performance requirements of the end-user. Also, we recommended that Management submit a written explanation for the lapse in providing item descriptions on various purchases.

We recommend that management make a written communication to all concerned employees to liquidate immediately all cash advances already due for liquidation pursuant to applicable COA Circular No.97-002 in order to inform them that they can no longer avail of additional cash advances unless the previous one is already liquidated and that wilful neglect to do same would constitute a valid cause for the management to withhold their salaries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already sent written communication to the concerned officers and employees calling for the immediate liquidation of cash advances. As of exit conference date, some of the unliquidated advances were already liquidated and the Municipal Accountant has strictly monitored cash advances, prohibiting the grant of new cash advances to employees with existing cash advances.
observations.reason: Some concerned employees still did not liquidate their cash advances after receiving written communication.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the lacking documents and observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2012-001 and COA Circular No. 2004-008 on the documentation requirements and proper recording of fund transfers.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR, Finding No. 3 submitted the lacking documents and adjusted the books to reflect proper recording of transfers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer adhere to the provisions of Section 15 and 25 of the NGAS Manual by enforcing remittance of collections by the collecting officers, intact and daily, to the Liquidating Officer and in case of noncompliance therewith, demand the collecting officers and penalize the erring collecting officers.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR, Finding No. 5 daily remittance of collections and penalized erring collecting officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that henceforth, the Municipal Accountant establish at the beginning of the year, receivables due for collection in the current year, with a corresponding credit to Deferred Real Property Tax Income, based on Real Property Tax Register/Taxpayer’s index card, as certified by the Municipal Treasurer, pursuant to Section 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR, Finding No. 6 set up Real Property Tax Receivables and Deferred Real Property Tax Income at the beginning of CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management use the 20% Development Fund only for projects and programs prescribed under DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-01. Otherwise, implementation of development projects, programs and activities that are not in conformity with JMC No. 2011-01 shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR, Finding No. 7 Implementation of 20% Development Projects and Programs. Management promised to use the 20% Development Fund Appropriation only for Projects and Programs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that henceforth, management stop the practice of charging to SEF transactions outside of the scope for which this fund was established and observe the proper utilization thereof pursuant to the provisions of DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular Nos. 1, series of 1998, 01-A series of 2000 and 01-B series of 2001 and Section100(c) of the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR, Finding No. 8 now observes proper charging to SEF transactions and promised to furnish supplemental Budget and Augmentation of Funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that henceforth, management exercise due diligence in the usage of funds in the implementation of programs, projects and activities, pursuant to Section 4 (1) of PD 1445 and Section 336 of RA 7160, that they may not exceed the appropriations to the detriment of other programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR, Finding No. 9 exercised due diligence in the usage of funds and furnished to COA all Supplemental Budget and Augmentation of Funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Bids and Awards Committee be mindful of compliance with the documentary requirements required in Items 9.1 and 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 and Items 23.1, 25.2 and 34.2 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184, in order to enhance transparency and accountability in the conduct of procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR, Finding No. 10 submitted the lacking supporting documents and promised to furnish complete supporting documents for future transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the GAD Focal Person of the Municipality immediately submit the GAD Accomplishment Report to COA. Further, we recommend that the Chairperson of GAD Focal Point System regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to timely evaluate those activities for implementation before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the municipal employees and general public pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA- NCPRW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR, Finding No. 14 submitted the CY 2016 GAD Accomplishment Report to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the GAD Focal Person to ensure allocation of at least five percent of the total annual budget of the LGU in compliance with DBM, NEDA and NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1 dated April 5, 2004 and Section B, Part 5 of the Updated Budget Operations Manual for Local Government Units.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR, Finding No. 15 management allocated five percent of the total annual budget of the LGU for CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management abide by the provisions of COA Circular No. 94-103 through the immediate utilization of the funds received, timely liquidation of all implemented projects and remittance of the remaining balances thereof to agencies or seek authority for the utilization of the excess funds.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 AAR, Finding No. 16 Management continue tracing of documents and coordination with source agencies; partially implemented.
observations.reason: The amount of Due to Other NGAs has increased as of CY 2016 due to funds received in CY 2016 from different sources; however, source documents for earlier balances were not promptly submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the BAC officials to actively participate in the procurement processes of the municipality in order to comply with Section 12, Rule V of R.A. 9184 and likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to diligently check attachments to the Disbursement Voucher to ensure completeness of all the necessary documents in compliance with Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 AAR, Finding No. 17 submitted the necessary supporting documents in CY 2016 for some disbursement vouchers.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents are still lacking for certain disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management minimize the incurrence of excessive and unnecessary catering for meals/snacks. If the same cannot be avoided, we recommend that management ensure that the meals, snacks, catering services for special occasions, conferences, and committee meetings are duly supported with guest lists or list of prospective attendees in order to arrive at the most proximate number of attendees. Likewise, we recommend that other cost of meals and snacks to be spent for entertaining visitors/guests of their corresponding offices be charged to the monthly representation allowance of department heads which is the very purpose of their allowance.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 AAR, Finding No. 18 Management has limited disbursement for meals and snacks in CY 2015 and CY 2016.
observations.reason: Compared to CY 2014 and CY 2015, the catering for meals and snacks were minimal in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Budget Officer to charge its appropriate expense item in the annual budget and the Municipal Accountant to record the wages of casual employees to its appropriate account – Salaries and Wages-Casual as prescribed in the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) chart of accounts. In addition, the Municipality should maximize the services of permanent employees so as not to exceed the 55% Personal Services Limitation and to minimize the hiring of casual employees as to necessity.
observations.managementAction: CY 2013 AAR, Finding No. 19 Management pledges to minimize the hiring of casual and job-order employees.
observations.reason: Management cited limited appropriation for Personal Services as the reason for continued hiring of job-order employees.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare weekly Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued to support the Journal Entry Voucher in recording the supplies and materials issued and maintain an updated Supplies Ledger Card and Stock Cards for each inventory item received.
observations.managementAction: CY 2011 AAR, Finding No. 21 No action taken; implementation is ongoing.
observations.reason: Management has yet to fully implement weekly preparation and submission of summaries to support inventory recordings.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter. Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the utilization of the funds released to these Leagues by requiring the recipient Leagues concerned to submit liquidation reports.
observations.managementAction: CY 2011 AAR, Finding No. 22 Management called the attention of the official regarding the non-issuance of official receipts of the Republic of the Philippines.
observations.reason: Some Leagues have yet to issue an official receipt of the Republic of the Philippines.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer conduct an information campaign to all real property owners within the municipality on the civil remedies that can be availed of by the municipality in the collection of taxes, as provided for in Sections 172 to 185 of RA 7160. This would create awareness that the owners may lose their properties due to failure to pay real property tax.
observations.managementAction: CY 2010 AAR, Finding No. 23 Management has actively sought out delinquent real property owners.
